skill.name: guard-regression
skill.description: デグレーション監視スキル（リファクタリング前後の品質比較、ロールバック判断）
skill.summary: デグレーション監視スキル（リファクタリング前後の品質比較、ロールバック判断）
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are partly false positives because the detector matched Markdown code fences, not Ruby backtick execution. The skill still directs agents to run local test, lint, build, coverage, and rollback commands, so publication should include an external command warning. The weak cryptography and entropy findings are false positives based on plain YAML and Japanese Markdown content.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Gradle and pnpm test, lint, build, and coverage commands, plus a git revert command in rollback guidance. These commands are appropriate for regression checks, but they execute project code and can change repository state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly lists local build and test commands under required regression checks. The intent appears legitimate, but the commands may run untrusted project scripts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding at the description line is not supported by the content. Line 3 contains a Japanese skill description and no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, or hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic primitive or weak algorithm at this line. The line is plain YAML metadata.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Obfuscated Content
low_findings.description: The entropy finding appears to be caused by Japanese text and Markdown formatting, not binary, encrypted, or intentionally hidden content. The file is readable instructions throughout.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is plain Markdown with visible headings, lists, and command examples. No encoded payloads or concealed instructions were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings identify Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. They are not executable code inside the skill file, although the fenced examples do document commands for an agent to run.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters or inline path formatting, not language-level execution syntax. The broader command workflow remains separately documented as medium risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Directed Bash Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill expects Bash access to run repository test, lint, build, and coverage commands. This pattern can trigger arbitrary behavior defined by the target repository's build scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly lists Bash as required and provides commands to execute. No evidence shows malicious intent, but execution risk is inherent.

content.value_statement: Refactoring can break tests, builds, coverage, and API behavior without clear warning. This skill records a baseline, repeats validation, compares results, and recommends rollback when regression appears.
actual_capabilities: Records backend and frontend baseline test results before refactoring.
Runs the same validation commands after refactoring for fair comparison.
Compares test success, lint errors, build results, and coverage changes.
Classifies severe and minor regressions using documented thresholds.
Produces verification reports with rollback recommendations.
Guides post-rollback validation against the original baseline.
limitations: Assumes Gradle and pnpm workflows match the target repository.
Requires permission to run local build and test commands.
Does not prove correctness beyond available tests and quality checks.
Rollback advice depends on accurate commit and baseline information.

use_cases.title: Protect a service refactor
use_cases.description: Compare backend test, build, lint, and coverage results before and after a service-layer refactor.

use_cases.title: Validate frontend cleanup
use_cases.description: Run frontend test, lint, build, and coverage checks after component cleanup to catch accidental behavior changes.

use_cases.title: Gate risky changes
use_cases.description: Create a clear go or rollback recommendation before merging a broad refactoring branch.

prompt_templates.title: Create a baseline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use guard-regression to record the current backend and frontend baseline. Capture test, lint, build, and coverage results.

prompt_templates.title: Check refactor results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use guard-regression to rerun validation after this refactor. Compare every result with the baseline and report any regression.

prompt_templates.title: Decide rollback status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use guard-regression to classify these validation changes as severe, minor, or safe. Recommend whether rollback is required.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a complex branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use guard-regression to review baseline and post-refactor evidence across backend and frontend checks. Produce a merge or rollback recommendation.

output_examples.input: Baseline checks passed, but one backend test fails after refactoring.
output_examples.output: The report marks a severe regression, identifies the failed test area, and recommends immediate rollback before merge.

output_examples.input: Frontend coverage drops by three percent, while tests and builds pass.
output_examples.output: The report marks a minor regression warning and asks for review before accepting the refactor.

output_examples.input: All checks match or improve after refactoring.
output_examples.output: The report states that no regression was found and allows the refactoring workflow to continue.

best_practices: Run baseline and post-refactor checks in the same environment.
Record numeric results instead of relying on general pass or fail notes.
Treat new test failures and build failures as rollback blockers.
anti_patterns: Do not compare results from different branches, machines, or dependency states.
Do not ignore baseline failures when judging new regressions.
Do not run rollback commands without confirming the target commit.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to run local test, lint, build, coverage, and rollback-related commands.

faq.question: Is it safe for any repository?
faq.answer: Use caution. Repository scripts can execute arbitrary local behavior, so review commands before approving them.

faq.question: What projects does it fit best?
faq.answer: It fits projects that use a Gradle backend and pnpm frontend validation workflow.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It supports quality gates, but humans should still review code, architecture, and product behavior.

faq.question: What counts as severe regression?
faq.answer: New test failures, failed builds, large coverage drops, and serious new lint errors are treated as severe.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the reviewed skill file. It focuses on local repository checks.
